title: Blackbox Tests: Inventory Commands
body:
## Blackbox Tests: Inventory Commands

**Type**: Test
**Wave**: 0

> **Wave 0 context**: This is a pre-implementation issue. The CLI is NOT implemented yet -- you are writing tests BEFORE implementation. Your tests will fail when run against the empty scaffold, and that is expected. Focus on encoding the specified behavior as test cases using the helpers API. Do NOT implement the CLI yourself.

### What to Read

**Testing methodology (read this before writing your test):**
- `docs/blackbox-testing.md` -- Best practices for writing robust CLI blackbox tests. Follow these patterns for flexible output parsing, assertions, partial credit, data isolation, and error case testing.

**Test framework (read this to understand the API you must use):**
- `scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- Shared test infrastructure already provided. Contains `run_cmd()`, `pass()`/`fail()`/`pass_partial()`, `assert_success()`/`assert_error()`/`assert_empty()`, `setup_cmd()`, `new_data_dir()`, scoring, and JSON output. Your file will be auto-sourced by `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` -- do NOT duplicate these functions.

> **Common mistakes (your PR will be rejected if you do any of these):**\n> - `run_cmd "inventory add espresso 100 ml"` -- WRONG (single string). Correct: `run_cmd inventory add espresso 100 ml` (separate args)\n> - `source helpers.sh` or `source scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- WRONG. helpers.sh is auto-sourced by the entry point. Do NOT source it yourself.\n> - `detect_cli` -- WRONG. Do NOT call detect_cli. It is called once by the entry point.\n> - `barista inventory list` -- WRONG. Use `run_cmd inventory list` (no `barista` prefix; `run_cmd` handles CLI detection).\n> - `return 0` or `exit 0` at the top of your test function -- WRONG (stub test). Your function must contain real `run_cmd`/`assert_*` calls that actually exercise the CLI.\n> - Calling both `assert_success "name" "cat"` and `pass "name" "cat"` for the same check -- WRONG (double-counting). `assert_success` already calls `pass` internally.\n> - `[[ "$OUTPUT_TEXT" == "espresso: 100 ml" ]]` -- FRAGILE. Use pattern matching (`espresso.*100.*ml`) to tolerate whitespace and formatting differences.

**Specification (the source of truth for expected behavior):**
- `docs/operational-specification.md` -- Read ONLY the Inventory Commands section and the Persistence section. Do NOT read the entire spec or other command sections.

### Requirements

- Create `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-inventory.sh` with test functions that use the helpers from `helpers.sh`
- Define a `test_inventory_commands()` function containing all your tests
- Register a category with `register_category "inventory" 25 "Inventory"` at the top of your file (outside the function)
- Test ALL inventory commands: `inventory list`, `inventory add`
- Test error cases: unit mismatch when adding with wrong unit for existing ingredient
- Test quantity accumulation (add same ingredient twice, verify total increases)
- Test empty inventory list (exits 0, no errors)
- Test data persistence across CLI invocations (add item, run list in new invocation, verify item present)
- Use `new_data_dir()` for test isolation between test groups. **WARNING:** Do NOT call `new_data_dir` inside `$()` (e.g., `data_dir=$(new_data_dir)`). The function exports `BARISTA_DATA_DIR` as a side-effect, and `$()` runs it in a subshell where the export is silently discarded. Call it bare: `new_data_dir` then use `$BARISTA_DATA_DIR`.
- Do NOT read all open issues or the full operational spec

### Files to Touch

- Create: `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-inventory.sh`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-inventory.sh` created and executable
- [ ] Tests all inventory CLI commands from the spec
- [ ] Tests all inventory error cases from the spec
- [ ] Uses helpers.sh framework (no duplicated helper functions)
- [ ] Uses data isolation via `new_data_dir()`
- [ ] PR created and merged

title: Blackbox Tests: Recipe Commands
body:
## Blackbox Tests: Recipe Commands

**Type**: Test
**Wave**: 0

> **Wave 0 context**: This is a pre-implementation issue. The CLI is NOT implemented yet -- you are writing tests BEFORE implementation. Your tests will fail when run against the empty scaffold, and that is expected. Focus on encoding the specified behavior as test cases using the helpers API. Do NOT implement the CLI yourself.

### What to Read

**Testing methodology (read this before writing your test):**
- `docs/blackbox-testing.md` -- Best practices for writing robust CLI blackbox tests. Follow these patterns for flexible output parsing, assertions, partial credit, data isolation, and error case testing.

**Test framework (read this to understand the API you must use):**
- `scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- Shared test infrastructure already provided. Contains `run_cmd()`, `pass()`/`fail()`/`pass_partial()`, `assert_success()`/`assert_error()`/`assert_empty()`, `setup_cmd()`, `new_data_dir()`, scoring, and JSON output. Your file will be auto-sourced by `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` -- do NOT duplicate these functions.

> **Common mistakes (your PR will be rejected if you do any of these):**\n> - `run_cmd "recipes add ..."` -- WRONG (single string). Correct: `run_cmd recipes add ...` (separate args)\n> - `source helpers.sh` or `source scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- WRONG. helpers.sh is auto-sourced by the entry point. Do NOT source it yourself.\n> - `detect_cli` -- WRONG. Do NOT call detect_cli. It is called once by the entry point.\n> - `barista recipes list` -- WRONG. Use `run_cmd recipes list` (no `barista` prefix; `run_cmd` handles CLI detection).\n> - `return 0` or `exit 0` at the top of your test function -- WRONG (stub test). Your function must contain real `run_cmd`/`assert_*` calls that actually exercise the CLI.\n> - Calling both `assert_success "name" "cat"` and `pass "name" "cat"` for the same check -- WRONG (double-counting). `assert_success` already calls `pass` internally.\n> - `[[ "$OUTPUT_TEXT" == "latte: 2 steps" ]]` -- FRAGILE. Use a plain substring or regex (`latte`) to tolerate whitespace and formatting differences.

**Specification (the source of truth for expected behavior):**
- `docs/operational-specification.md` -- Read ONLY the Recipe Commands section. Do NOT read the entire spec or other command sections.

### Requirements

- Create `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-recipes.sh` with test functions that use the helpers from `helpers.sh`
- Define a `test_recipe_commands()` function containing all your tests
- Register a category with `register_category "recipes" 25 "Recipes"` at the top of your file (outside the function)
- Test ALL recipe commands: `recipes list`, `recipes show`, `recipes add`
- Test recipe creation with `--name`, `--ingredient`, and `--step` flags
- Test duplicate recipe name error
- Test missing ingredients error (recipe must have at least 1 ingredient)
- Test missing steps error (recipe must have at least 1 step)
- Test recipe not-found error (`recipes show nonexistent`)
- Test recipe lookup by name
- Use `new_data_dir()` for test isolation between test groups. **WARNING:** Do NOT call `new_data_dir` inside `$()` (e.g., `data_dir=$(new_data_dir)`). The function exports `BARISTA_DATA_DIR` as a side-effect, and `$()` runs it in a subshell where the export is silently discarded. Call it bare: `new_data_dir` then use `$BARISTA_DATA_DIR`.
- Do NOT read all open issues or the full operational spec

### Files to Touch

- Create: `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-recipes.sh`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-recipes.sh` created and executable
- [ ] Tests all recipe CLI commands from the spec
- [ ] Tests all recipe error cases from the spec
- [ ] Uses helpers.sh framework (no duplicated helper functions)
- [ ] Uses data isolation via `new_data_dir()`
- [ ] PR created and merged

title: Blackbox Tests: Order Commands
body:
## Blackbox Tests: Order Commands

**Type**: Test
**Wave**: 0

> **Wave 0 context**: This is a pre-implementation issue. The CLI is NOT implemented yet -- you are writing tests BEFORE implementation. Your tests will fail when run against the empty scaffold, and that is expected. Focus on encoding the specified behavior as test cases using the helpers API. Do NOT implement the CLI yourself.

### What to Read

**Testing methodology (read this before writing your test):**
- `docs/blackbox-testing.md` -- Best practices for writing robust CLI blackbox tests. Follow these patterns for flexible output parsing, assertions, partial credit, data isolation, and error case testing.

**Test framework (read this to understand the API you must use):**
- `scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- Shared test infrastructure already provided. Contains `run_cmd()`, `pass()`/`fail()`/`pass_partial()`, `assert_success()`/`assert_error()`/`assert_empty()`, `setup_cmd()`, `new_data_dir()`, `extract_id()`, scoring, and JSON output. Your file will be auto-sourced by `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` -- do NOT duplicate these functions.

> **Common mistakes (your PR will be rejected if you do any of these):**\n> - `run_cmd "order place ..."` -- WRONG (single string). Correct: `run_cmd order place ...` (separate args)\n> - `source helpers.sh` or `source scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- WRONG. helpers.sh is auto-sourced by the entry point. Do NOT source it yourself.\n> - `detect_cli` -- WRONG. Do NOT call detect_cli. It is called once by the entry point.\n> - `barista order place ...` -- WRONG. Use `run_cmd order place ...` (no `barista` prefix; `run_cmd` handles CLI detection).\n> - `return 0` or `exit 0` at the top of your test function -- WRONG (stub test). Your function must contain real `run_cmd`/`assert_*` calls that actually exercise the CLI.\n> - Calling both `assert_success "name" "cat"` and `pass "name" "cat"` for the same check -- WRONG (double-counting). `assert_success` already calls `pass` internally.\n> - `[[ "$OUTPUT_TEXT" == "Order placed: abc-123" ]]` -- FRAGILE. Use a substring or regex (`Order.*placed` or `abc-123`) to tolerate whitespace and formatting differences.

**Specification (the source of truth for expected behavior):**
- `docs/operational-specification.md` -- Read the Order Commands section (`order place`, `order validate`, `order brew`), the Orders List section (`orders list`, `--status` filter), and the Size Scaling section (S=1.0x, M=1.5x, L=2.0x). Do NOT read the entire spec or other command sections.

### Requirements

- Create `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-orders.sh` with test functions that use the helpers from `helpers.sh`
- Define a `test_order_commands()` function containing all your tests
- Register a category with `register_category "orders" 25 "Orders"` at the top of your file (outside the function)
- Test `order place` with recipe name and `--size` flag
- Test `order validate` state transition (PLACED -> VALIDATED)
- Test `order brew` state transition (VALIDATED -> COMPLETED) and brew plan output
- Test `orders list` and `orders list --status COMPLETED` filter
- Test size scaling: place orders with S, M, L sizes
- Test invalid state transitions (e.g., validate an already-brewed order)
- Test ordering a nonexistent recipe (error)
- Test brewing without validating first (error, per spec)
- Use `extract_id()` from helpers to capture order IDs from command output
- Use `setup_cmd()` to seed inventory and recipes before testing orders
- Use `new_data_dir()` for test isolation between test groups. **WARNING:** Do NOT call `new_data_dir` inside `$()` (e.g., `data_dir=$(new_data_dir)`). The function exports `BARISTA_DATA_DIR` as a side-effect, and `$()` runs it in a subshell where the export is silently discarded. Call it bare: `new_data_dir` then use `$BARISTA_DATA_DIR`.
- Do NOT read all open issues or the full operational spec

### Files to Touch

- Create: `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-orders.sh`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-orders.sh` created and executable
- [ ] Tests all order CLI commands from the spec
- [ ] Tests all order error cases from the spec
- [ ] Tests size scaling with all three sizes (S, M, L)
- [ ] Uses helpers.sh framework (no duplicated helper functions)
- [ ] Uses data isolation via `new_data_dir()`
- [ ] PR created and merged

title: Blackbox Tests: End-to-End Workflows
body:
## Blackbox Tests: End-to-End Workflows

**Type**: Test
**Wave**: 0

> **Wave 0 context**: This is a pre-implementation issue. The CLI is NOT implemented yet -- you are writing tests BEFORE implementation. Your tests will fail when run against the empty scaffold, and that is expected. Focus on encoding the specified behavior as test cases using the helpers API. Do NOT implement the CLI yourself.

### What to Read

**Testing methodology (read this before writing your test):**
- `docs/blackbox-testing.md` -- Best practices for writing robust CLI blackbox tests. Follow these patterns for flexible output parsing, assertions, partial credit, data isolation, and error case testing.

**Test framework (read this to understand the API you must use):**
- `scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- Shared test infrastructure already provided. Contains `run_cmd()`, `pass()`/`fail()`/`pass_partial()`, `assert_success()`/`assert_error()`/`assert_empty()`, `setup_cmd()`, `new_data_dir()`, `extract_id()`, scoring, and JSON output. Your file will be auto-sourced by `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` -- do NOT duplicate these functions.

> **Common mistakes (your PR will be rejected if you do any of these):**\n> - `run_cmd "inventory add espresso 100 ml"` -- WRONG (single string). Correct: `run_cmd inventory add espresso 100 ml` (separate args)\n> - `source helpers.sh` or `source scripts/blackbox-tests/helpers.sh` -- WRONG. helpers.sh is auto-sourced by the entry point. Do NOT source it yourself.\n> - `detect_cli` -- WRONG. Do NOT call detect_cli. It is called once by the entry point.\n> - `barista inventory list` -- WRONG. Use `run_cmd inventory list` (no `barista` prefix; `run_cmd` handles CLI detection).\n> - `return 0` or `exit 0` at the top of your test function -- WRONG (stub test). Your function must contain real `run_cmd`/`assert_*` calls that actually exercise the CLI.\n> - Calling both `assert_success "name" "cat"` and `pass "name" "cat"` for the same check -- WRONG (double-counting). `assert_success` already calls `pass` internally.\n> - `[[ "$OUTPUT_TEXT" == "espresso: 100 ml" ]]` -- FRAGILE. Use pattern matching (`espresso.*100.*ml`) to tolerate whitespace and formatting differences.

**Specification (the source of truth for expected behavior):**
- `docs/operational-specification.md` -- Read the User Workflows section. This section describes multi-step scenarios that chain commands together. Also skim the Persistence section for data isolation requirements. Do NOT read other sections in detail.

### Requirements

- Create `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-workflows.sh` with test functions that use the helpers from `helpers.sh`
- Define a `test_workflow_commands()` function containing all your tests
- Register a category with `register_category "workflows" 25 "Workflows"` at the top of your file (outside the function)
- Test full order lifecycle: add inventory -> add recipe -> place order -> validate -> brew -> verify state
- After brewing, verify inventory was consumed (quantities decreased by correct scaled amounts)
- Test insufficient inventory rejection: drain inventory, then verify validation rejects
- Test data persistence: write data in one CLI invocation, read it back in another
- Test round-trip verification: create a resource, immediately read it back and verify it exists
- Use `extract_id()` from helpers to capture IDs from command output and pass to subsequent commands
- Use `setup_cmd()` to seed inventory and recipes, aborting on failure
- Use `new_data_dir()` for each test group to prevent state pollution. **WARNING:** Do NOT call `new_data_dir` inside `$()` (e.g., `dir=$(new_data_dir)`). The function exports `BARISTA_DATA_DIR` as a side-effect, and `$()` runs it in a subshell where the export is silently discarded. Call it bare: `new_data_dir` then use `$BARISTA_DATA_DIR`.
- Do NOT read all open issues or the full operational spec

### Files to Touch

- Create: `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-workflows.sh`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] `scripts/blackbox-tests/test-workflows.sh` created and executable
- [ ] Tests end-to-end workflows from the spec
- [ ] Tests inventory consumption after brewing
- [ ] Tests insufficient inventory handling
- [ ] Tests data persistence across CLI invocations
- [ ] Uses helpers.sh framework (no duplicated helper functions)
- [ ] Uses data isolation via `new_data_dir()`
- [ ] PR created and merged

title: Test: CLI Commands Interface Contract
body:
## Test: CLI Commands Interface Contract

**Type**: Test
**Layer**: Interface
**Wave**: 1

### Test Specification

**What to test**: Create interface contract tests for all CLI commands. Define expected input/output formats, error message formats, and exit codes.

**Operational spec reference**: `docs/operational-specification.md` (CLI Commands section)
**Interface contract**: `contracts/cli-commands.yaml` (to be created)

**Resilience**: Since implementation happens in parallel across multiple workers and PRs, contract tests must be resilient to incremental implementation. Use conditional skip markers (e.g., `pytest.importorskip`, `@pytest.mark.skipif`) for command groups not yet registered. Tests should validate what exists without hard-failing on what doesn't.

**Expected behaviors**:
- [ ] CLI command interfaces defined in `contracts/cli-commands.yaml`
- [ ] All command argument parsing tested
- [ ] Output format consistency verified
- [ ] Error message formats verified
- [ ] Exit codes verified (0 for success, non-zero for errors)

### Access Restrictions

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: Test implementation code should NOT be accessible to implementation workers.
- Workers should only see this test specification
- Workers should implement based on operational spec, not test code
- Test results (pass/fail) will validate implementation

### Files to Touch

- Create: `contracts/cli-commands.yaml`
- Create: `tests/interfaces/cli-commands-contract.test.py`
- Update: `scripts/check.sh` (add interface tests)

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Contract file created (`contracts/cli-commands.yaml`)
- [ ] Interface tests created in `tests/interfaces/cli-commands-contract.test.py`
- [ ] Tests cover all behaviors listed
- [ ] Tests are documented
- [ ] Tests integrated into `check.sh`
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Test implementation is NOT exposed to implementation workers
- [ ] `./scripts/check.sh` passes

title: Implement: Domain Entities
body:
## Implement: Domain Entities

**Type**: Implementation
**Wave**: 1
**Depends on**: #5, #6

### Primary Goal

**Implement per operational specification** (`docs/operational-specification.md`): Domain entities section
**Deliver value**: Core business logic entities with validation and business rules.

### Implementation Guidance

**DO**:
- ✅ Read operational specification at `docs/operational-specification.md` (domain entities section)
- ✅ Read `specs/plan.md` (Domain Layer section)
- ✅ Write unit tests first (TDD), then implement
- ✅ Focus on delivering value per the specification
- ✅ Ensure `pyproject.toml` has a `[project.scripts]` entry: `barista = "robotic_barista.cli:cli"` so `barista` works after `pip install -e .`
- ✅ Create `src/robotic_barista/cli/__main__.py` that imports and calls the CLI so `python -m robotic_barista.cli` works

**DO NOT**:
- ❌ Access test implementation code from #5 or #6
- ❌ Implement to pass tests; implement to meet specification
- ❌ Look at test code to understand requirements

### Test Validation

Tests from #5 and #6 will validate your implementation.
- Tests are validation tools, not implementation guides
- If tests fail, check your implementation against the operational spec at `docs/operational-specification.md`
- If you believe a test is wrong, create issue with label `blocker:test-arbitration`

### Files to Touch

- Create: `src/robotic_barista/domain/ingredient.py`
- Create: `src/robotic_barista/domain/inventory_item.py`
- Create: `src/robotic_barista/domain/recipe.py`
- Create: `src/robotic_barista/domain/order.py`
- Create: `tests/unit/domain/test_size_scaling.py`
- Create: `tests/unit/domain/test_order_state_machine.py`
- Create: `tests/unit/domain/test_inventory_validation.py`
- Update: `pyproject.toml` (add `[project.scripts]` entry for `barista`)
- Create: `src/robotic_barista/cli/__main__.py`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation matches operational specification (PRIMARY)
- [ ] `barista` CLI entry point works after `pip install -e .`
- [ ] All entities created with proper validation
- [ ] Size scaling logic implemented (S=1.0x, M=1.5x, L=2.0x)
- [ ] Order state machine implemented
- [ ] Unit tests written (TDD) and passing
- [ ] All interface contract tests from #5 and #6 pass
- [ ] Code coverage >= 60%
- [ ] `ruff check` passes with no errors
- [ ] `ruff format --check .` passes (code is formatted)
- [ ] `./scripts/check.sh` passes
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] PR includes test evidence
- [ ] Review verifies value delivery, not just test passing

title: Implement: JSON Storage
body:
## Implement: JSON Storage

**Type**: Implementation
**Wave**: 1
**Depends on**: #8

### Primary Goal

**Implement per operational specification** (`docs/operational-specification.md`): Persistence section
**Deliver value**: JSON file storage with atomic writes, ensuring data integrity.

### Implementation Guidance

**DO**:
- ✅ Read operational specification at `docs/operational-specification.md` (persistence section)
- ✅ Read `specs/plan.md` (Data Model section)
- ✅ Implement atomic write pattern (temp file → rename)
- ✅ Write unit tests first (TDD), then implement
- ✅ Write integration tests for persistence
- ✅ Focus on delivering value per the specification

**DO NOT**:
- ❌ Use in-memory storage
- ❌ Skip atomic writes (data corruption risk)

### Files to Touch

- Create: `src/robotic_barista/storage/json_repository.py`
- Create: `tests/unit/storage/test_json_repository.py`
- Create: `tests/integration/test_persistence.py`

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Implementation matches operational specification (PRIMARY)
- [ ] JSON storage works correctly
- [ ] Atomic writes prevent corruption (temp file → rename)
- [ ] Data directory created on first use
- [ ] Unit tests written (TDD) and passing
- [ ] Integration tests for persistence passing
- [ ] Code coverage >= 60%
- [ ] `ruff check` passes with no errors
- [ ] `ruff format --check .` passes (code is formatted)
- [ ] `./scripts/check.sh` passes
- [ ] Documentation updated
- [ ] PR includes test evidence

title: Implement: Recipe CLI Commands
body:
## Implement: Recipe CLI Commands

**Type**: Implementation
**Wave**: 2
**Depends on**: #10

### Primary Goal

**Implement per operational specification** (`docs/operational-specification.md`): Recipe CLI commands section

title: Test: Integration Tests
body:
## Test: Integration Tests

**Type**: Test
**Layer**: Integration
**Wave**: 3
**Depends on**: #18, #19

### Test Specification

**What to test**: Create integration tests for complete workflows.

**Operational spec reference**: `docs/operational-specification.md` (User Workflows section)

**Blackbox test reference**: Review `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` (if it exists) for additional context on expected CLI behaviors and workflows. Use it to inform your integration test design.

**Expected behaviors**:
- [ ] Complete order workflow (place → validate → brew)
- [ ] Persistence across operations
- [ ] Cross-component interactions
- [ ] Error scenarios

### Access Restrictions

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: Test implementation code should NOT be accessible to implementation workers.
- Workers should only see this test specification
- Workers should implement based on operational spec, not test code
- Test results (pass/fail) will validate implementation

### Files to Touch

- Create: `tests/integration/test_order_workflow.py`
- Create: `tests/integration/test_persistence.py`
- Create: `tests/integration/test_inventory_recipe_order.py`
- Update: `scripts/check.sh` (add integration tests)

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Integration tests created
- [ ] Tests verify complete workflows
- [ ] Tests verify persistence
- [ ] Tests verify cross-component interactions
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Tests integrated into `check.sh`
- [ ] Test implementation is NOT exposed to implementation workers

title: Test: Black Box System Tests
body:
## Test: Black Box System Tests

**Type**: Test
**Layer**: System (Black Box)
**Wave**: 3
**Depends on**: #18, #19

### Test Specification

**What to test**: Create black box system tests derived from operational specification.

**Operational spec reference**: `docs/operational-specification.md` (entire document - all CLI commands, workflows, behaviors)

**Blackbox test reference**: Review `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` (if it exists) for additional context on expected CLI behaviors, workflows, and error cases. Use it to inform your system test design.

**Expected behaviors**:
- [ ] All CLI commands as black box (execute via subprocess)
- [ ] Complete user workflows end-to-end
- [ ] Error scenarios
- [ ] Edge cases
- [ ] Output format matches spec exactly
- [ ] Exit codes verified (0 for success, non-zero for errors)

### Access Restrictions

⚠️ **IMPORTANT**: These tests are derived from operational specification.
- Test implementation code should NOT be accessible to implementation workers
- Workers should only see this test specification
- Workers should implement based on operational spec, not test code
- Test results (pass/fail) will validate implementation

### Files to Touch

- Create: `tests/system/black-box/cli-commands.test.py`
- Create: `tests/system/black-box/user-workflows.test.py`
- Update: `scripts/check.sh` (add system tests)

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Black box tests created in `tests/system/black-box/cli-commands.test.py`
- [ ] Tests derived from operational specification
- [ ] Tests verify all CLI commands as black box
- [ ] Tests verify complete user workflows
- [ ] Tests verify error scenarios
- [ ] Tests verify output format matches spec exactly
- [ ] Tests verify exit codes
- [ ] Tests pass
- [ ] Tests integrated into `check.sh`
- [ ] Test implementation is NOT exposed to implementation workers

title: Test: Comprehensive Test Coverage
body:
## Test: Comprehensive Test Coverage

**Type**: Test
**Wave**: 4
**Depends on**: #21

### Description

Ensure comprehensive test coverage across all layers.

**Blackbox test reference**: Review `scripts/blackbox-test.sh` (if it exists) for context on expected CLI behaviors and edge cases that should be covered by your tests.

**Important**: Coverage is measured by `pytest-cov`. Subprocess-based black-box tests (e.g., tests that shell out to `barista` via `subprocess.run`) do NOT contribute to coverage measurement. Focus on unit and integration tests that import and call Python code directly for coverage improvement. You may exclude subprocess test files from coverage targets if needed.

### Files to Touch

- Review all test files
- Add missing test cases
- Add unit/integration tests for uncovered code paths
- Update coverage thresholds

### Definition of Done

- [ ] Code coverage >= 80% (measured by pytest-cov; subprocess-based tests do not count)
- [ ] All test layers have adequate coverage
- [ ] Edge cases covered
- [ ] `./scripts/check.sh` passes
